questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: Compute the average of the four bar values shown: $4, 6, 8, 10$.

figure.caption: Bar chart of four values
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 220 150' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><line x1='30' y1='120' x2='210' y2='120' stroke='#475569'/><g fill='#bfdbfe' stroke='#475569'><rect x='45' y='90' width='25' height='30'/><rect x='85' y='75' width='25' height='45'/><rect x='125' y='60' width='25' height='60'/><rect x='165' y='45' width='25' height='75'/></g><text x='55' y='138' font-size='8'>4, 6, 8, 10</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Average $=\dfrac{\sum x}{n}$.

**Working.**
$$\bar{x}=\frac{4+6+8+10}{4}=\frac{28}{4}=7$$

**Answer.** $7$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: A sector representing $25\%$ of a total is shown shaded on the pie chart. Compute its central angle (degrees).

figure.caption: Pie sector
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 170 170' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><circle cx='85' cy='80' r='60' fill='#e5e7eb' stroke='#475569'/><path d='M85 80 L85 20 A60 60 0 0 1 145 80 Z' fill='#2563eb'/><text x='30' y='158' font-size='8'>shaded = 25% of pie</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Central angle $=\text{fraction}\times360^\circ$.

**Working.**
$$\theta=0.25\times360=90^\circ$$

**Answer.** $90^\circ$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: Select the correct article: 'The supervisor is ____ honest engineer who never compromises on safety.'
questions.solution:
**Concept.** The choice between 'a' and 'an' depends on the sound, not the spelling, of the following word.

**Working.** 'Honest' begins with a silent 'h', so it starts with a vowel sound, requiring 'an'.

**Answer.** an.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: The area of a rectangle of length $12$ and width $5$ is _____ .
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Area of rectangle $=\text{length}\times\text{width}$.

**Working.** $12\times 5 = 60$.

**Answer.** $60$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: Choose the correct comparative form: 'This seam is ____ than the one we mined last year.'
questions.solution:
**Concept.** One-syllable adjectives form the comparative with '-er', and 'than' signals a comparative (not superlative).

**Working.** 'Thick' becomes 'thicker'; 'more thicker' is a double comparative and 'thickest' is superlative.

**Answer.** thicker.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: A invests $12000$ for $6$ months and B invests $9000$ for $8$ months. Their profit-sharing ratio A : B is
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Share is proportional to (capital $\times$ time).

**Working.** A $=12000\times 6=72000$; B $=9000\times 8=72000$; ratio $=1:1$.

**Answer.** $1:1$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: Choose the correct word: 'The blast had a serious ____ on the surrounding structures.'
questions.solution:
**Concept.** 'Effect' is the noun (a result); 'affect' is usually the verb (to influence).

**Working.** A noun is needed after 'a serious', so 'effect' is correct.

**Answer.** effect.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: A man travels to a town at $40$ km/h and returns along the same road at $60$ km/h. His average speed for the whole journey is _____ km/h.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** For equal distances, average speed is the harmonic mean.

**Working.**
$$\frac{2\times 40\times 60}{40+60}=\frac{4800}{100}=48$$

**Answer.** $48$ km/h.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: X is the brother of the son of Y's son. How is X related to Y?
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Resolve generations from the inside out.

**Working.** 'The son of Y's son' is Y's grandson; X is his brother, hence also Y's grandson.

**Answer.** Grandson.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: The volume of a cylinder of radius $7$ and height $10$ is _____ (use $\pi=22/7$).
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Volume $=\pi r^2 h$.

**Working.** $\dfrac{22}{7}\times 49\times 10 = 22\times 7\times 10 = 1540$.

**Answer.** $1540$.

questions.subject: Solid & Hazardous Waste Management
questions.stem: Wet MSW $1000\,\text{kg}$ at $50\%$ moisture. The dry-solids mass is _____ kg.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Dry = wet × (1 − moisture).

**Working.**
$$1000(1-50/100)=500.$$

**Answer.** $500\,\text{kg}$

questions.subject: Air & Noise Pollution
questions.stem: At a receptor, $3$ identical machines each produce $85\,\text{dB}$. The combined sound pressure level is _____ dB.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** $L=L_1+10\log_{10}n$ for $n$ equal incoherent sources.

**Working.**
$$85+10\log_{10}3=89.77.$$

**Answer.** $89.77\,\text{dB}$

questions.subject: Environmental Chemistry
questions.stem: First-order decay, $k=0.1\,\text{d}^{-1}$. Half-life is _____ days.

questions.subject: Environmental Microbiology
questions.stem: Drinking-water standards require E. coli to be ABSENT in any 100 mL sample. A membrane-filtration test of a 100 mL sample of treated water yields 8 E. coli colonies. The correct interpretation is that the water
options: fails the potability standard and is unsafe to drink
is potable, since 8 is a low count
questions.solution:
**Concept.** The standard is zero E. coli per 100 mL; 8 colonies/100 mL exceeds it, signalling recent faecal contamination — the water is unsafe.

**Working.**
Compare the measured count to the zero-tolerance standard.

**Answer.** Fails / unsafe

questions.subject: Solid & Hazardous Waste Management
questions.stem: Decomposable waste of $1000\,\text{kg}$ yields landfill gas at $0.15\,\text{m}^3/\text{kg}$. The total gas generated is _____ m³.

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Gas $=$ mass $\times$ specific yield.

**Working.**
$$1000\times0.15=150.$$

**Answer.** $150\,\text{m}^3$

questions.subject: Global & Regional Environmental Issues
questions.stem: A source emits $6\,\text{t}$ of a greenhouse gas. Reading the gas's GWP₁₀₀ from the figure, its CO₂-equivalent emission is _____ t CO₂-e.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 300 130" width="100%" style="max-width:300px;height:auto"><rect x="40" y="36" width="220" height="56" rx="6" fill="rgba(217,119,6,0.12)" stroke="#1e293b"/><text x="150" y="58" font-size="11" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">the gas</text><text x="150" y="80" font-size="12" text-anchor="middle" fill="#dc2626">GWP₁₀₀ = 265</text><text x="150" y="112" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">CO₂-e = mass × GWP</text></svg>
figure.caption: Global-warming potential of the gas marked on the diagram only. (read values from the figure)

questions.solution:
**Concept.** CO₂-e = mass × GWP, with the GWP read from the figure.

**Working.**
$$6\times265=1590.$$

**Answer.** $1590$

questions.subject: Air & Noise Pollution
questions.stem: $10$ identical sources each $80\,\text{dB}$. Combined level is _____ dB.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** L = L₁ + 10log n.

**Working.**
$$80+10\log10=90.$$

**Answer.** $90\,\text{dB}$

questions.subject: Water & Wastewater Treatment
questions.stem: Ammonia-bearing water is chlorinated. From the breakpoint-chlorination curve shown, read the breakpoint dose. To leave a free residual of $1.5\,\text{mg/L}$, the chlorine that must be applied is _____ mg/L.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 330 195" width="100%" style="max-width:350px;height:auto"><line x1="38" y1="20" x2="38" y2="165" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><line x1="38" y1="165" x2="312" y2="165" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><text x="4" y="30" font-size="8" fill="#1e293b">residual</text><text x="232" y="182" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">Cl₂ dose (mg/L) →</text><path d="M38 165 L95 120 L150 150 L205 160 L300 80" fill="none" stroke="#2563eb" stroke-width="1.7"/><line x1="205" y1="55" x2="205" y2="165" stroke="#dc2626" stroke-width="1" stroke-dasharray="3 2"/><circle cx="205" cy="160" r="3" fill="#dc2626"/><text x="170" y="50" font-size="9" fill="#dc2626">breakpoint</text><text x="186" y="178" font-size="9" fill="#dc2626">6</text><text x="250" y="92" font-size="7" fill="#16a34a">free residual</text></svg>
figure.caption: Breakpoint dose marked on the x-axis only. (read values from the figure)

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Beyond the breakpoint, extra chlorine appears as free residual: applied = breakpoint dose + desired free residual.

**Working.**
$$6+1.5=7.5.$$

**Answer.** $7.5\,\text{mg/L}$

**Concept.** v_s = depth ÷ time (read both from the trajectory).

**Answer.** $6.25\,\text{mm/s}$

questions.subject: Environmental Microbiology
questions.stem: In a reactor, $30\,\text{mg/L}$ of biomass is produced while $120\,\text{mg/L}$ of substrate is consumed. The observed yield $Y_{X/S}$ is _____ mg/mg.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** $Y_{X/S}=\dfrac{\Delta X}{\Delta S}$.

**Working.**
$$\dfrac{30}{120}=0.25.$$

**Answer.** $0.25$

questions.subject: Environmental Management & Ethics
questions.stem: Select all statutes/instruments that are central environmental legislation in India.
options: The Water (Prevention and Control of Pollution) Act, 1974
The Air (Prevention and Control of Pollution) Act, 1981
questions.solution:
**Concept.** The Water Act (1974), Air Act (1981) and EP Act (1986) are the core environmental statutes; the Companies Act is corporate law.

**Working.**
First three are environmental law.

**Answer.** Water, Air and EP Acts

questions.subject: Environmental Microbiology
questions.stem: Bacteria grow exponentially with specific growth rate $\mu=0.231\,\text{h}^{-1}$. The doubling (generation) time is _____ hours.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 330 180" width="100%" style="max-width:350px;height:auto"><line x1="38" y1="18" x2="38" y2="158" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><line x1="38" y1="158" x2="312" y2="158" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><text x="6" y="28" font-size="8" fill="#1e293b">ln X</text><text x="270" y="174" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">time →</text><path d="M38 150 L80 148 L150 60 L230 40 L300 95" fill="none" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="1.7"/><text x="50" y="142" font-size="7" fill="#1e293b">lag</text><text x="105" y="100" font-size="7" fill="#16a34a">log (μ_max)</text><text x="215" y="34" font-size="7" fill="#1e293b">stationary</text><text x="278" y="110" font-size="7" fill="#dc2626">death</text></svg>
figure.caption: Batch microbial growth curve.

questions.solution:
**Concept.** $t_d=\dfrac{\ln 2}{\mu}$.

**Working.**
$$\dfrac{0.693}{0.231}=3.$$

**Answer.** $3\,\text{h}$

questions.subject: Global & Regional Environmental Issues
questions.stem: A source emits $3\,\text{t}$ of a greenhouse gas. Reading the gas's GWP₁₀₀ from the figure, its CO₂-equivalent emission is _____ t CO₂-e.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 300 130" width="100%" style="max-width:300px;height:auto"><rect x="40" y="36" width="220" height="56" rx="6" fill="rgba(217,119,6,0.12)" stroke="#1e293b"/><text x="150" y="58" font-size="11" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">the gas</text><text x="150" y="80" font-size="12" text-anchor="middle" fill="#dc2626">GWP₁₀₀ = 265</text><text x="150" y="112" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">CO₂-e = mass × GWP</text></svg>
figure.caption: Global-warming potential of the gas marked on the diagram only. (read values from the figure)

questions.solution:
**Concept.** CO₂-e = mass × GWP, with the GWP read from the figure.

**Working.**
$$3\times265=795.$$

**Answer.** $795$

questions.subject: Environmental Management & Ethics
questions.stem: At a discount rate of $6\%$, and reading the future benefit $F$ and its time horizon $n$ from the cash-flow diagram, the present worth is $₹$ _____.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 330 150" width="100%" style="max-width:350px;height:auto"><line x1="30" y1="100" x2="310" y2="100" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.4"/><text x="30" y="118" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">0</text><line x1="290" y1="100" x2="290" y2="48" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="1.6"/><path d="M290 46 l-5 9 10 0 z" fill="#16a34a"/><text x="290" y="40" font-size="10" text-anchor="middle" fill="#16a34a">F = ₹80,000</text><text x="290" y="118" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">year 3</text><text x="165" y="118" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">time (years) →</text><text x="42" y="60" font-size="10" fill="#dc2626">PW = ?</text></svg>
figure.caption: Future benefit F and its time horizon n marked on the cash-flow diagram only. (read values from the figure)

questions.solution:
**Concept.** PW = F/(1+i)^n, with F and n read from the diagram.

**Working.**
$$\dfrac{80000}{(1+0.06)^{3}}=67170.$$

**Answer.** $₹67170$

questions.subject: Environmental Microbiology
questions.stem: A culture follows Monod kinetics at substrate $S=40\,\text{mg/L}$. Reading $\mu_{max}$ and $K_s$ from the $\mu$–$S$ curve shown, the specific growth rate $\mu$ is _____ h⁻¹.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 330 195" width="100%" style="max-width:350px;height:auto"><line x1="44" y1="20" x2="44" y2="165" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><line x1="44" y1="165" x2="312" y2="165" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><text x="240" y="182" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">substrate [S] →</text><line x1="44" y1="40" x2="312" y2="40" stroke="#dc2626" stroke-width="1" stroke-dasharray="4 3"/><text x="232" y="36" font-size="10" fill="#dc2626">μ_max = 0.4</text><path d="M44 165 C90 110 140 70 200 56 C250 46 290 42 312 41" fill="none" stroke="#2563eb" stroke-width="1.8"/><line x1="44" y1="102" x2="120" y2="102" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="0.8" stroke-dasharray="3 2"/><line x1="120" y1="102" x2="120" y2="165" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="0.8" stroke-dasharray="3 2"/><text x="14" y="106" font-size="7" fill="#16a34a">μ_max/2</text><text x="98" y="178" font-size="10" fill="#16a34a">K_s = 50</text></svg>
figure.caption: Monod μ–S curve: μ_max (plateau) and K_s (half-saturation) marked on the curve only. (read values from the figure)

questions.solution:
**Concept.** μ = μ_max S/(K_s+S), with μ_max and K_s read from the curve.

**Working.**
$$\dfrac{0.4\times40}{50+40}=0.178.$$

**Answer.** $0.178$

questions.subject: Environmental Microbiology
questions.stem: A culture follows Monod kinetics. Reading $\mu_{max}$ (plateau) and the half-saturation constant $K_s$ from the $\mu$–$S$ curve shown, the specific growth rate at substrate $S=60\,\text{mg/L}$ is _____ h⁻¹.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 330 195" width="100%" style="max-width:350px;height:auto"><line x1="44" y1="20" x2="44" y2="165" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><line x1="44" y1="165" x2="312" y2="165" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><text x="240" y="182" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">substrate [S] →</text><line x1="44" y1="40" x2="312" y2="40" stroke="#dc2626" stroke-width="1" stroke-dasharray="4 3"/><text x="232" y="36" font-size="10" fill="#dc2626">μ_max = 0.4</text><path d="M44 165 C90 110 140 70 200 56 C250 46 290 42 312 41" fill="none" stroke="#2563eb" stroke-width="1.8"/><line x1="44" y1="102" x2="120" y2="102" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="0.8" stroke-dasharray="3 2"/><line x1="120" y1="102" x2="120" y2="165" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="0.8" stroke-dasharray="3 2"/><text x="14" y="106" font-size="7" fill="#16a34a">μ_max/2</text><text x="98" y="178" font-size="10" fill="#16a34a">K_s = 20</text></svg>
figure.caption: Monod μ–S curve: μ_max (plateau) and K_s (half-saturation) marked on the curve only. (read values from the figure)

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Monod: $\mu=\dfrac{\mu_{max}S}{K_s+S}$, with $\mu_{max}$ and $K_s$ read from the curve.

**Working.**
$$\dfrac{0.4\times60}{20+60}=0.3.$$

**Answer.** $0.3\,\text{h}^{-1}$

questions.subject: Solid & Hazardous Waste Management
questions.stem: Modified Dulong $HHV=337C+1419(H-O/8)$ kJ/kg with $C=40\%$, $H=6\%$, $O=40\%$. HHV is _____ kJ/kg.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 300 180" width="100%" style="max-width:320px;height:auto"><line x1="40" y1="150" x2="280" y2="150" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><rect x="70" y="30" width="46" height="120" fill="#dc2626" opacity="0.75"/><text x="93" y="26" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle" fill="#dc2626">in 14.9</text><rect x="190" y="146" width="46" height="4" fill="#16a34a" opacity="0.8"/><text x="213" y="142" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle" fill="#16a34a">out 0</text><text x="160" y="170" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">HHV (MJ/kg)</text></svg>
figure.caption: Energy content of the waste fuel.

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Apply the modified Dulong formula.

**Working.**
$$337(40)+1419(6-40/8)=14899.$$

**Answer.** $14899\,\text{kJ/kg}$

questions.subject: Water & Wastewater Treatment
questions.stem: Compared with a slow sand filter, a rapid sand filter is characterised by
options: higher filtration rate and reliance on prior coagulation
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Rapid sand filters run at ~100–125 times the slow-filter rate, need coagulation–sedimentation upstream, and are cleaned by backwashing.

**Working.**
Higher rate, needs coagulation.

**Answer.** High rate, coagulation-dependent

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 330 150" width="100%" style="max-width:350px;height:auto"><line x1="30" y1="100" x2="310" y2="100" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.4"/><text x="30" y="118" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">0</text><line x1="290" y1="100" x2="290" y2="48" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="1.6"/><path d="M290 46 l-5 9 10 0 z" fill="#16a34a"/><text x="290" y="40" font-size="10" text-anchor="middle" fill="#16a34a">F = ₹50,000</text><text x="290" y="118" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">year 3</text><text x="165" y="118" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">time (years) →</text><text x="42" y="60" font-size="10" fill="#dc2626">PW = ?</text></svg>
figure.caption: Future benefit F and its time horizon n marked on the cash-flow diagram only. (read values from the figure)

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Present worth $=\dfrac{F}{(1+i)^n}$, with $F$ and $n$ read from the cash-flow diagram.

**Working.**

**Answer.** $₹37566$

questions.subject: Air & Noise Pollution
questions.stem: An ESP treats $6\,\text{m}^3/\text{s}$. Reading the collecting area $A$ and drift velocity $w$ from the figure, its collection efficiency is _____ %.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 340 170" width="100%" style="max-width:360px;height:auto"><rect x="40" y="30" width="260" height="110" fill="none" stroke="#1e293b" stroke-width="1.2"/><line x1="80" y1="30" x2="80" y2="140" stroke="#94a3b8" stroke-width="3"/><line x1="150" y1="30" x2="150" y2="140" stroke="#94a3b8" stroke-width="3"/><line x1="220" y1="30" x2="220" y2="140" stroke="#94a3b8" stroke-width="3"/><line x1="115" y1="35" x2="115" y2="135" stroke="#dc2626" stroke-width="1" stroke-dasharray="2 2"/><line x1="185" y1="35" x2="185" y2="135" stroke="#dc2626" stroke-width="1" stroke-dasharray="2 2"/><path d="M14 70 l22 0 m-6 -3 l6 3 -6 3" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="1.2" fill="none"/><text x="6" y="64" font-size="9" fill="#16a34a">gas in</text><text x="60" y="158" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">collecting area A = 150 m²</text><text x="150" y="22" font-size="9" fill="#dc2626">w = 0.08 m/s (drift)</text></svg>
figure.caption: Collecting area A and drift velocity w marked on the diagram only. (read values from the figure)

questions.solution:
**Concept.** η = 1 − e^{−wA/Q}, with A and w read from the figure.

**Working.**
$$(1-e^{-0.08\times150/6})\times100=86.47\%.$$

**Answer.** $86.47\%$

questions.subject: Ecology & Biodiversity
questions.stem: A population grows logistically at $r=0.4\,\text{yr}^{-1}$. Reading the carrying capacity $K$ from the growth curve shown, the maximum $dN/dt$ ($rK/4$) is _____ /yr.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 320 190" width="100%" style="max-width:340px;height:auto"><line x1="34" y1="20" x2="34" y2="165" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><line x1="34" y1="165" x2="305" y2="165" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><line x1="34" y1="40" x2="305" y2="40" stroke="#dc2626" stroke-width="1" stroke-dasharray="4 3"/><text x="240" y="36" font-size="9" fill="#dc2626">K = 10000</text><path d="M34 158 C120 156 150 80 175 60 C210 44 260 41 305 40" fill="none" stroke="#2563eb" stroke-width="1.8"/><text x="10" y="30" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">N</text><text x="285" y="180" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">time →</text><text x="120" y="120" font-size="8" fill="#16a34a">max dN/dt at N=K/2</text></svg>
figure.caption: Carrying capacity K marked on the growth curve only. (read values from the figure)

questions.solution:
**Concept.** max dN/dt = rK/4 at N=K/2, with K read from the curve.

**Working.**
$$\dfrac{0.4\times10000}{4}=1000.$$

**Answer.** $1000$
